revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:弁財天宮]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Benzaiten Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Arikawa-benten3.JPG |location = {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|qid=Q1206790|lt=Shin-Kamigotō|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Arikawa-go, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|32.985556|N|129.118806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|田霧姫神|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagiri-hime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |Rank = Former Unranked shrine |Established = Genroku 4 (1691) |Other names = Benten |Festivals = festival (February 12th) |Events = Benzaiten Festival (3rd Sunday in January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Benzaiten Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} }} '''Benzaiten Shrine''' (弁財天宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|qid=Q1206790|lt=Shin-Kamigotō|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Arikawa-go, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. This article also describes the related '''Benzaiten Festival'''. == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|田霧姫神|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagiri-hime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. == History == In Kanbun 1 (1661), a maritime boundary dispute arose between the {{ill|Gotō Domain|ja|五島藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福江藩|1=Fukue Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3034437}} and the newly separated Tomie territory of the Fukue Domain. The headman of six Arikawa villages, Eguchi Jin’emon, petitioned both domains for a resolution but without success. In Jōkyō 4 (1687), he filed a lawsuit in Edo. The Edo judgment was delayed, requiring Jin’emon to visit Edo three times. During his stays, he prayed at 96 shrines and temples. In Genroku 3 (1690), Arikawa won the case. On his return, Jin’emon brought back a division of spirit from the {{ill|Kamakura Benzaiten|ja|宇賀福神社|1=Zeniarai Benzaiten Ugafuku Shrine|lt=Zeniarai Benzaiten Ugafuku Shrine|2=ja|3=宇賀福神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銭洗弁財天宇賀福神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q642681}} and enshrined it at a sacred site in the northeastern "kimon" direction of Arikawa. The shrine was also made the protector deity of the whaling organization {{ill|Arikawa Whaling Guild|ja|有川鯨組|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Whaling Guild|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A worship hall was later built on the current shrine site. In 1908, the shrine was merged with the local {{ill|Arikawa Shrine|ja|有川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Shrine|qq=}}. == Rituals == ; Major Festivals * festival (February 12th) == Benzaiten Festival == The '''Benzaiten Festival''' is a traditional event in the Arikawa district that honors the shrine's deity. It is held in mid-January and includes traditional performances. == Overview == The festival features prayers for bountiful harvests, prosperity, family fortune, and whale memorial services. Songs and drum performances last 5–10 minutes. Youth groups and students from six districts (Hama, Funatsu, Nakasuji, Takasaki, Kami-Arikawa, and Nishihara) visit local shrines, public offices, and households throughout the day. Originally held on January 14th, the festival date was changed in 2009 to the Sunday after the fire department’s new year ceremony. In 2012, it moved again to the preceding Saturday. === History === In Genroku 6 (1693), the Arikawa Whaling Guild began operations with 11 Year ceremonies featuring whale songs and dances. By the late Meiji period, the decline of whaling led to the festival’s disappearance. In 1930, the festival was revived during a prefectural youth event and reintroduced as an annual tradition. === Whale Songs === Five of the ten traditional whale songs are still performed today. Each district maintains unique lyrics, rhythms, and drumming styles. Common songs include: * "Benzaiten" * "Ōuta" (Grand Song) * "Kiuuta" (Life Song) * "Toshi no Hajime" (11 Year’s Beginning) * "Danna-sama" (Lord) == References == * Arikawa Town History Compilation Committee, ''Arikawa Town Historical Records'', Heisei 6 * Fumirō Araki, ''The Facts and Myths of Arikawa’s History'', Heisei 16 {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|弁財天宮|version=87467800|comment=Imported full ja history}}
